title: Dregs
content:
The fire is out, and spent the warmth thereof
    (This is the end of every song man sings!)
    The golden wine is drunk, the dregs remain,
    Bitter as wormwood and as salt as pain;
    And health and hope have gone the way of love
    Into the drear oblivion of lost things.
    Ghosts go along with us until the end;
    This was a mistress, this, perhaps, a friend.
    With pale, indifferent eyes, we sit and wait
    For the dropt curtain and the closing gate:
    This is the end of all the songs man sings.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ernest Christopher Dowson, titled "Dregs", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
